raw_artifact_policy: Record immutable fingerprints and review decisions. Do not redistribute the supplied article text or figures; curated facts are stored separately with primary-source provenance.

artifacts.reason: The claimed 1.2 mM lactate/default-250 mM protocol and universal 5/5 result conflict with the repository's four-way lineage audit. The current vendored executable remains 2/5; only the non-vendored legacy lineage reaches 5/5 under recovered 0.8 mM lactate and an actually selected 276.6667 mM glycogen grid point.
Primary Kemas 2021 article text was used to restore Table 1 time windows, SDs, exact media conditions and downstream insulin responses.

The supplied 25 mM high-glucose label is wrong (primary source: 11 mM), low insulin is 0.1 nM rather than about 1 nM, low-insulin conditions also contain 100 nM glucagon, and the supplied rows omitted SD and 0-6 h window semantics.
The primary source supports a matched insulin exposure and pAKT/PCK1/G6PC responses, but no INSR surface abundance, receptor occupancy, EC50, Hill coefficient or complete kinetic trajectory.
No numeric in-situ human sinusoidal pO2 or compartment-resolved human redox ratio is established. Human liver MPS data support directional zone functions under controlled 3-13% oxygen only.
All numeric rows are model predictions or a model setpoint; the only requested human comparator is NULL. Exact model hash, complete boundaries and a reproduced generation protocol are also absent.
Retained in the existing hepatic-flux evidence layer with original organ, splanchnic and systemic denominators.
Missing quantities remain explicit blockers and are not imputed.
Coverage visualization only; scientific records come from the primary sources and curated tables.
Derived visualization; its labels are superseded by the primary-source condition audit.
The plotted trajectories are model outputs without a human held-out comparator and cannot be called a held-out validation curve.

review_findings: A delivered contract is not a source of truth when it conflicts with reproduced local artifacts.
The Kemas PHH spheroid observations are valid same-format validation targets, not fresh-suspension or in-vivo single-hepatocyte parameters.
Hormone-bundle contrasts in Kemas cannot be interpreted as pure insulin effects because glucagon supplementation changes at the same time.
Wilson hepatocellularity supports a human organ-to-cell denominator but does not establish single-hepatocyte geometry.
No delivered numeric trajectory qualifies as independent held-out human validation.
No human in-situ zonal oxygen value is promoted into the simulation.
